title: moehringia dasyphylla subsp. sedoides
intro: Moehringia dasyphylla subsp. sedoides, also known as Sedum-leaved Milkwort, is a perennial plant species within the family Caryophyllaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, as it contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and menstrual cramps, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to relax the muscles and soothe the digestive system. In herbal preparations, Moehringia dasyphylla subsp. sedoides is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote digestive well-being and calm the nervous system.
